_readme: Phase 9. Unlike FalcoMoveData.json (hand-transcribed from fightcore.gg
screenshots), these numbers come directly from MeleeLight's real
open-source engine source (src/characters/fox/attributes.js hitbox
definitions + src/characters/fox/moves/*.js timer logic) -- an actual
shipped implementation, not a screenshot transcription. Cross-reference
against a real PlFx.dat/PlFxAJ.dat parse when that's available.
UNITS: frame numbers are 1-indexed action frames (frame the move's own
internal timer reaches when hitboxes.active flips true/false), matching
FalcoMoveData.json's convention so both files read the same way.
Multi-hit moves (nair/fair/bair/uair/upsmash/etc. have 2-5 real MeleeLight
hit instances) are collapsed to ONE representative hit -- the first/
strongest hitbox id0 -- same simplification FalcoMoveData.json and
MoveDef (Characters/MoveData.cs) both already make; see that file's
doc comment. IASA here is approximate: several of these move files only
have a single post-active interrupt check rather than FalcoMoveData's
separate 'early IASA vs full end' pair, so iasa is set a few frames
before totalFrames rather than pulled from a second distinct check --
harmless since PlayerMover.TickAttacking doesn't consume IasaFrame yet
(see its doc comment).
Side-B (Illusion) is deliberately OMITTED: real Melee Illusion has no
attack hitbox against a grounded opponent, so there's nothing to
transcribe. Up-B (Fire Fox) and Down-B (Reflector) ARE included for
completeness even though PlayerMover.TryStartAttack only ever dispatches
the special button to NeutralB right now (see its doc comment) -- so
neither is reachable in the current build, but the data is real and
ready for whenever that gap closes.
NeutralB is NOT here: the hybrid design (Docs/CHARACTERS.md) uses
Falco's Blaster for Neutral B, not Fox's own laser -- see FalcoMoveData.json's
blasterGround/blasterAir instead.

attributesSummary._source: Characters/Fox/FoxAttributes.cs (real PlFx.dat + MeleeLight attributes.js, cross-validated) -- summarized here only, that file is the source of truth

fireBird._note: Up-B (Fire Fox) launch hit. Not dispatched by input yet -- see _readme.

reflectorHit._note: Down-B (Reflector) direct-contact hit. Not dispatched by input yet -- see _readme.
